differential_diagnoses: Drug-induced neurotoxicity (e.g., opioids, gabapentin, or deferasirox)
next_steps: Urgent workup for secondary hypertension (e.g., renal function, creatinine, urinalysis)
Lumbar puncture to rule out infectious etiologies
Avoidance of neurotoxic or seizure-threshold lowering medications
Evaluation for occult systemic infection or inflammation
Assessment for medication-related adverse effects or toxicities

differential_diagnoses: Symptomatic epilepsy (secondary to structural brain injury)
Renal-related secondary hypertension and hypertensive emergency
next_steps: Initiate continuous video EEG monitoring to assess for non-convulsive status epilepticus
Gradual control of blood pressure using short-acting, titratable intravenous agents
Perform lumbar puncture to assess for pleocytosis, protein, and infectious etiologies
Comprehensive metabolic panel and assessment of renal function (BUN, creatinine, electrolytes including Ca, Mg, PO4)
Ophthalmologic examination for hypertensive retinopathy or papilledema
Consultation with hematology and neurology for stabilization of sickle cell disease and seizure management
Review and discontinuation of potential neurotoxic or seizure-threshold lowering medications
